instruction: Generate a JSON function call to send an email. The function is called 'send_email' and takes parameters: to (string), subject (string), body (string), cc (array of strings, optional), priority (string: low/normal/high).
input: Send an email to john@company.com with subject 'Q1 Report Ready' saying the quarterly report is attached. CC the finance team at finance@company.com. Mark it as high priority.
output: {"function": "send_email", "arguments": {"to": "john@company.com", "subject": "Q1 Report Ready", "body": "The quarterly report is attached for your review.", "cc": ["finance@company.com"], "priority": "high"}}

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to create a calendar event. The function is called 'create_event' and takes parameters: title (string), start_time (string, ISO datetime), end_time (string, ISO datetime), attendees (array of strings), location (string), reminder_minutes (integer).

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to translate text. The function is called 'translate_text' and takes parameters: text (string), source_language (string, ISO 639-1 code), target_language (string, ISO 639-1 code), formality (string: formal/informal).

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to generate a report. The function is called 'generate_report' and takes parameters: report_type (string), date_from (string), date_to (string), format (string: pdf/csv/xlsx), include_charts (boolean), email_to (string, optional).
input: Generate a PDF sales report for January 2025 with charts and email it to manager@company.com.

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to set a reminder. The function is called 'set_reminder' and takes parameters: message (string), remind_at (string, ISO datetime), recurring (boolean), recurrence_pattern (string: daily/weekly/monthly, optional), priority (string: low/medium/high).
input: Remind me every Monday at 9am to submit my weekly status report. High priority.

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to analyze text sentiment. The function is called 'analyze_sentiment' and takes parameters: text (string), language (string), granularity (string: document/sentence), include_entities (boolean).

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to create a GitHub issue. The function is called 'create_issue' and takes parameters: repo (string, format: owner/repo), title (string), body (string), labels (array of strings), assignees (array of strings), milestone (string, optional).
input: Create an issue on acme/backend-api titled 'Fix timeout on /users endpoint'. Describe that the endpoint times out after 5s under load. Label it 'bug' and 'performance'. Assign to devteam-alice. Add to milestone v3.1.
output: {"function": "create_issue", "arguments": {"repo": "acme/backend-api", "title": "Fix timeout on /users endpoint", "body": "The /users endpoint times out after 5 seconds under load. Needs investigation and optimization.", "labels": ["bug", "performance"], "assignees": ["devteam-alice"], "milestone": "v3.1"}}

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to schedule a batch job. The function is called 'schedule_batch_job' and takes parameters: job_name (string), cron_expression (string), script_path (string), timeout_seconds (integer), retries (integer), environment_vars (object).
input: Schedule a nightly data sync job called 'sync-orders' at 2am UTC. It runs /scripts/sync.py with a 1-hour timeout and 3 retries. Set DB_HOST to prod-db.internal.

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to search for nearby places. The function is called 'search_places' and takes parameters: query (string), latitude (number), longitude (number), radius_meters (integer), type (string: restaurant/hotel/gas_station/hospital), max_results (integer).

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to send a Slack notification. The function is called 'send_slack_message' and takes parameters: channel (string), message (string), blocks (array, optional), thread_ts (string, optional), unfurl_links (boolean).

instruction: Generate a JSON function call to run a machine learning prediction. The function is called 'predict' and takes parameters: model_id (string), input_data (object), return_probabilities (boolean), explain (boolean), timeout_ms (integer).
